trade.summary: Korea-South Africa trade is driven by Korean manufacturing exports and South African mineral exports. Korea exports automobiles (Hyundai, Kia have significant South African market share), electronics, and steel to South Africa. South Africa exports platinum group metals (critical for Korean automotive catalysts and fuel cells), chromium, iron ore, and coal to Korea. POSCO and other Korean steelmakers rely on South African chrome ore.

diplomatic.status: Neutral — amicable but limited strategic alignment
diplomatic.summary: Korea-South Africa diplomatic relations are friendly but limited in strategic depth. South Africa's BRICS membership and non-aligned foreign policy diverge from Korea's US-alliance orientation. South Africa abstained on UN resolutions condemning Russia's Ukraine invasion — at odds with Korea. Korea is an important trade and investment partner for South Africa. K-pop and Korean culture have growing followings in South Africa.
recent_incidents: South Africa's BRICS membership and non-aligned posture limits convergence with Korea's Western-oriented foreign policy

military.summary: Minimal defense relationship. No formal military alliance. South Africa maintains non-aligned defense posture. Korea has marketed defense equipment but no significant contracts have materialized. KOICA runs some capacity-building programs.

financial.summary: Korean FDI in South Africa is modest, led by Hyundai/Kia vehicle assembly, Samsung Electronics, and POSCO steel procurement activities. South African investment in Korea is negligible. The PGM import relationship creates strategic material dependency for Korea's battery and automotive industries.

energy.summary: South Africa exports some coal to Korea. Growing Korean interest in South Africa's green hydrogen potential and mineral resources (lithium, manganese for batteries). Korea's renewable energy companies are exploring South African project opportunities.

scientific.summary: Limited but growing scientific cooperation. KOICA development programs include education and technology transfer components. Growing cooperation on critical minerals processing technology given Korea's battery and semiconductor supply chain needs. Academic exchanges are modest.
